question: How did Amazon's net sales change in 2020?

passages.text: Amazon reported net sales of $386 billion in 2020, an increase of 38% over 2019.
The COVID-19 pandemic drove a surge in online shopping during 2020.

answer.text: Amazon's net sales increased 38% in 2020, reaching $386 billion.
Online shopping surged during the pandemic.

label: faithful

question: What is the boiling point of water at sea level?

passages.text: At standard atmospheric pressure (sea level), water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.
Atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, lowering water's boiling point.

answer.text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

label: faithful

question: Who wrote the novel 1984 and when was it published?

passages.text: George Orwell wrote the dystopian novel 1984, which was published in 1949.
Aldous Huxley wrote Brave New World, published in 1932.

answer.text: 1984 was written by George Orwell and published in 1949.

label: faithful

passages.text: While most swans are white, black swans are native to Australia, so not all swans are white.

answer.text: Not all swans are white; black swans are found in Australia.

question: Did the new policy increase processing fees?

passages.text: Under the 2023 policy, processing fees rose from 1.5% to 2.0% per transaction.
